hints.text: Wedge bollards w/ **rectangular ORANGE front reflector** + white back (thin version exists)

shared_with.diff: Orange rectangle → Lithuania (see the Baltic bollard infographic).

hints.text: Square concrete poles w/ **diagonal support beam** (rarely EE/LV); post-Soviet poletops (short rods, trident arrangements)

shared_with.diff: Much commoner in Lithuania; õ/ü language → Estonia.

hints.text: Chevrons white w/ red arrows — small single chevrons have a **red edge outline** (LV/PL versions lack it; EE/RU/UA = inverted colours)
Baltic blue km markers: Lithuania's at a **45° angle, L-shaped**; warning-sign borders: thin red + thin white outline; **A-type guardrails w/ ORANGE reflectors** (EE: none, LV: red/white)
Pedestrian signs **3 stripes, low-detail person** (RU/UA same; EE more detailed; LV = 5 stripes); bus-stop sign w/ **4-window bus** (LV: 5 windows; EE same as LT)
**Lithuanian**: hooked vowels **Ą Ę Į Ų** + **Ė** (dot) + Y; street = **gatve (g.)** ≈ Lithuania-only (ultra-rare 'gat.' in Riga); many towns end **-ai**

shared_with.diff: Hooked vowels + Ė → Lithuanian; Latvian uses macrons (ā ē ī ū) + iela.

hints.text: Light **brick buildings w/ steep corrugated metal roofs**; wooden houses (fewer than Estonia); pastel stone in suburbs; **Vytis flag** (knight on dark red) occasionally
Directional signs blue/white (Latvian arrowheads more stylised)
**Least forest of the Baltics** — more open & agricultural (use w/ caution)
Gen3: **random small blurs** on signs/objects (also Kaliningrad; not EE/LV); Gen4 black or red car (not in Estonia)

shared_with.note: Kaliningrad has the same random blurs.
No random blurs, no black/red Gen4 cars there.
shared_with.diff: Those metas → Lithuania (or Latvia for the cars).

hints.text: Fairly flat (nothing over 300 m) — biggest hills SE + central west; **metal pole tags → east**
**Curonian Spit** (road 167): N-S Gen4 road through Baltic-pine forest w/ windswept trees + red Scandinavian-look wooden houses
Rare **Polish bilingual signs** → small area in the southeast
